mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent upward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a spike in core inflation erodes corporate profit margins while forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, jointly depressing equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint GDPC1-CPIAUCSL composite signal remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its twelve-month moving average accelerates capacity utilization, which historically boosts industrial and cyclical equity earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically during post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 12-month average during the first 18 months of a recovery.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp deceleration in consumer credit demand and construction activity, which historically precedes equity drawdowns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite HOUST dropping below 1.2 million.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with declining real consumer spending indicates an unsustainable labor market driven by credit expansion rather than fundamental income growth, which ultimately triggers a mean-reverting equity selloff.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-RSAFS divergence signal produces a positive Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the consumer price index reflects entrenched inflationary pressure that forces higher terminal interest rates, directly compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not significantly decline when CPIAUCSL rises for three consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates beyond 3.5 percent annualized, it typically overheats the economy, prompting aggressive Federal Reserve tightening that subsequently crushes equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5 percent annualized.
